summary: Review #1 feedback from sourcery-ai[bot] correctly addressed: (1) CI test-race job now delegates to `make test-race RACE_FLAGS="-count=1"` instead of duplicating inline go test commands, establishing the Makefile as single source of truth for the module list; (2) OPERATOR usage comment clarified to reference the existing global override mechanism at Makefile lines 13-16; (3) all four shorthand conditions in docs/reference/ci-workflow.md DAG aligned with actual YAML expressions. No existing CI jobs modified, no new issues introduced.

tests_checklist.item: Makefile test-race target uses OPERATORS iteration pattern matching existing test target
CI delegates to make test-race, ensuring module list stays in sync
RACE_FLAGS defaults to empty for local use, CI passes -count=1
No existing CI job modified (diff shows only test-race block changes)
No job's needs array references test-race (grep confirmed)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing OPERATORS for-loop pattern from test target
RACE_FLAGS variable uses idiomatic Make conditional default (?=)
Comment accurately documents OPERATOR override mechanism with line reference
CI job uses SHA-pinned actions matching other jobs (checkout@de0fac2e..., setup-go@4b73464b...)
Docs DAG conditions match actual YAML expressions verbatim

security_checklist.item: No secrets or credentials in changed files
No user input vectors (RACE_FLAGS is set by developer/CI, not external input)

architecture_checklist.item: Single source of truth: module list defined only in Makefile OPERATORS variable
CI-specific flags (count=1) passed via RACE_FLAGS, not hardcoded in Makefile
test-race job remains independent (not in any other job's needs array)

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated module lists between CI and Makefile (sourcery-ai feedback addressed)
RACE_FLAGS mechanism is minimal — one variable, no over-engineering

fail_fast_checklist.item: for-loop uses || exit 1 to fail on first race detection
No continue-on-error or if: always() on the run step

defensive_checklist.item: RACE_FLAGS defaults to empty string — safe for local use without arguments

metadata.title: Add race condition detection to Go tests
